summary: Review cycle #4 addresses berendt external review #3 feedback (W-001, I-001, I-002) on the CC-0078 finalizer implementation. The MariaDB CR kind list is hoisted to a package-level mariaDBResourceCtors var in reconcile_database.go and iterated from both finalizeDatabaseResources and hasLiveMariaDBResources, eliminating the drift risk the original detect-duplicated-parallel-resource-lists pattern warned about. A mariaDBResourceKey(keystone) helper centralises the (Name, Namespace) derivation and is used from both the cleanup sites and buildDatabase/buildUser/buildGrant. A new multi-pass test, TestReconcile_TerminatingCR_EmitsFinalizingDatabaseEventOnlyOnce, exercises two consecutive Reconcile passes against a Keystone CR held in etcd by a foreign finalizer (MariaDB CRs kept Terminating by pending-delete finalizers) and asserts FinalizingDatabase is emitted exactly once, closing the gap flagged by I-001.

tests_checklist.item: Tests exist and pass (go test ./internal/controller/ passes in 1.051s)
New multi-pass test verifies FinalizingDatabase emitted exactly once across two Reconcile passes (I-001)
Existing finalizer tests unchanged and still pass
Edge cases (brownfield, idempotency, NotFound tolerance) still covered by prior tests

code_quality_checklist.item: Single source of truth for MariaDB CR kinds (mariaDBResourceCtors)
Single source of truth for CR naming (mariaDBResourceKey)
Helper functions documented with CC-0078 + REQ references
No code duplication between cleanup and sentinel paths
Follows existing condition-constant / helper-hoisting style in reconcile_database.go

security_checklist.item: No new user input surfaces introduced
gosec scan clean on changed files

architecture_checklist.item: Addresses W-001 drift risk with a single package-level var
mariaDBResourceKey centralises the naming convention for both create and cleanup paths
Solution is as simple as possible — two small helpers, no new abstractions

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated MariaDB CR kind list
No duplicated (Name, Namespace) construction for CRs
No speculative generality — helpers used by every current call site

fail_fast_checklist.item: Errors from Get/Delete wrapped with %T %s context
NotFound handled explicitly (skip vs early-return-true in sentinel)

defensive_checklist.item: Finalizer removal still only runs after Delete issued on all three kinds
Sentinel still gates FinalizingDatabase event on live-CR check

suggested_improvements: TestReconcile_TerminatingCR_EmitsFinalizingDatabaseEventOnlyOnce relies on the first pass removing the Keystone finalizer, so the second pass exits early via the ContainsFinalizer guard rather than actually exercising the hasLiveMariaDBResources sentinel on a retained finalizer. The test still catches the documented regression ('re-emit on every requeue would flood events'), but to also cover the sentinel path specifically — e.g. an Update-conflict retry where the keystoneFinalizer persists — consider a future variant that injects an Update conflict on the first pass and then asserts the second pass observes DeletionTimestamp-set MariaDB CRs and suppresses the event.
